summary:
The inferior oblique muscle elevates, abducts, and externally rotates the eyeball. It does NOT depress or rotate the globe inward; thus, downward rotation is an incorrect attribution and represents the exception among its actions.

Note that this summary focuses only on clinically accurate facts related directly to the action of the superior vs inferioir oblique muscles as per standard ophthalmology knowledge.
